children.heading: .—Boards of Officers To Examine Naval Vessels
children.label: a .—Boards of Officers To Examine Naval Vessels

children.text: The Secretary of the Navy shall designate boards of naval officers to examine naval vessels, including unfinished vessels, for the purpose of making a recommendation to the Secretary as to which vessels, if any, should be stricken from the Naval Vessel Register. Each vessel shall be examined at least once every three years if practicable.

children.label: A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any naval vessel examined under this section on or after , shall be examined with minimal notice provided to the crew of the vessel.January 1, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a vessel undergoing necessary trials before acceptance into the fleet.

children.heading: .—Actions by Board
children.label: b .—Actions by Board
children.text: A board designated under subsection (a) shall submit to the Secretary in writing its recommendations as to which vessels, if any, among those it examined should be stricken from the Naval Vessel Register.

children.heading: .—Action by Secretary
children.label: c .—Action by Secretary
children.text: If the Secretary concurs with a recommendation by a board that a vessel should be stricken from the Naval Vessel Register, the Secretary shall strike the name of that vessel from the Naval Vessel Register.

children.label: A
children.text: An overall narrative summary of the material readiness of Navy ships as compared to established material requirements standards.

children.label: B
children.text: The overall number and types of vessels inspected during the preceding fiscal year.

children.label: C
children.text: For in-service vessels, material readiness trends by inspected functional area as compared to the previous five years.

children.label: B
children.text: an unclassified form that is releasable to the public without further redaction.
